campbell.title: Campbell City Council — September 2, 2025
campbell.summary: The Campbell City Council met to review the city's finances for July 2025, including how the city's investments performed and a detailed treasurer's report on money in and out. The council also discussed a new plan to improve transportation options around Campbell, like making it easier to get around by bus, bike, or walking.
keyTopics: July 2025 investment performance and financial transactions
City treasurer's report on budget and spending
New multimodal transportation plan to improve buses, biking, and walking options

saratoga.title: Saratoga City Council — March 18, 2026
saratoga.summary: The Saratoga City Council held a public meeting on March 18, 2026, where residents had a chance to speak about a proposed new rule or law. After hearing from the public, the council introduced an ordinance (a new city rule) and skipped the usual first reading step to move things along faster. They directed staff to add it to the consent calendar so it can be officially approved at the next regular council meeting.
keyTopics: Public hearing held for resident input on proposed ordinance
First reading of ordinance waived to expedite process
Ordinance scheduled for final adoption at next regular council meeting
Staff directed to add measure to consent calendar

los-gatos.title: Los Gatos City Council — April 7, 2026
los-gatos.summary: The Los Gatos City Council met on April 7, 2026, to approve meeting minutes from several March sessions and authorize the Town Manager to sign a construction contract with QLM, Inc. The agenda was primarily administrative, focusing on record-keeping from previous meetings and moving forward with a construction project.
keyTopics: Approved minutes from six different March meetings and sessions
Authorized construction contract with QLM, Inc.
Multiple closed-door and public session meetings documented

san-jose.title: San José City Council — April 7, 2026
san-jose.summary: The San José City Council met on April 7, 2026, to approve routine meeting minutes and discuss several projects improving the city. The council approved a state grant for the Mexican Heritage Plaza's arts and culture school, moved forward with a traffic signal project on Bascom Avenue, and awarded a contract to repair and replace pavement on local streets.
keyTopics: State grant funding for Mexican Heritage Plaza arts programs
Bascom Avenue traffic signal improvements at railroad crossings
Local street pavement repair and replacement project
Approval of meeting records and committee minutes

mountain-view.title: Mountain View City Council — April 7, 2026
mountain-view.summary: The Mountain View City Council held a meeting on April 7, 2026 that included a virtual option for residents who wanted to participate online or by phone. Residents could join via Zoom or phone to speak during public comment periods on agenda items.
keyTopics: Virtual meeting access via Zoom and phone dial-in
Public comment procedures for remote participants
How to raise hand and unmute to speak during the meeting

sunnyvale.title: Sunnyvale City Council — April 7, 2026
sunnyvale.summary: The Sunnyvale City Council held a meeting on April 7, 2026, where they opened with the Pledge of Allegiance and recognized two important observances: Arab-American Heritage Month and Fair Housing Month. These recognitions highlight the city's commitment to celebrating diverse communities and ensuring equal housing opportunities for all residents.
keyTopics: Arab-American Heritage Month recognition
Fair Housing Month observance
Council meeting proceedings and public agenda

cupertino.title: Cupertino City Council — April 7, 2026
cupertino.summary: The Cupertino City Council met to discuss progress on the city's Active Transportation Plan, which focuses on making it easier and safer for people to walk and bike around town. The council also recognized April as both Donate Life Month and Fair Housing Month with official proclamations, and residents had the opportunity to speak about local issues during public comment.
keyTopics: Active Transportation Plan Phase 2 updates and revised scoring criteria for projects
Changes and edits made to the transportation plan since last review
Donate Life Month proclamation
Fair Housing Month proclamation
Public comment period for resident concerns

santa-clara.title: Santa Clara City Council — April 7, 2026
santa-clara.summary: Santa Clara City Council held a meeting on April 7, 2026, using both in-person and online options so residents could participate however they preferred. The council provided multiple ways for the public to join and submit comments about agenda items.
keyTopics: Hybrid meeting format available (in-person and Zoom)
Remote participation via Zoom video conference
Phone dial-in option for residents without internet
eComments submission process for written public input

milpitas.title: Milpitas City Council — April 7, 2026
milpitas.summary: The Milpitas City Council met on April 7, 2026 to discuss updates to the city's long-term housing and community development plan (2025-2030), review requests from council members for new projects to work on, and consider changes to how the city fills council vacancies. The council also approved meeting minutes and looked ahead to upcoming agenda items.
keyTopics: Updates to the city's 5-year housing and development plan
New project requests from individual council members
Changes to how council vacancies are filled
Review of upcoming meeting schedules and agendas

palo-alto.title: Palo Alto City Council — March 31, 2026
palo-alto.summary: The Palo Alto City Council reviewed and approved the financial plans for the city's gas utility service for the next fiscal year. This included approving changes to how much residents and businesses will pay for natural gas, adjusting reserve funds, and transferring money between accounts to keep the utility running smoothly.
keyTopics: Gas rate changes for residential customers (G-1 schedule)
Gas rate changes for commercial and master-metered buildings (G-2 schedule)
Gas rate changes for large commercial users (G-3 schedule)
Financial reserves and transfers between utility and general city funds
